name: @radzor/background-job
description: Persistent background job queue with retries, scheduling, and concurrency control. Built on BullMQ (Redis) or pg-boss (PostgreSQL). Supports delayed jobs, cron-scheduled jobs, priority queues, and dead letter queues.

inputs.name: driver
inputs.description: Queue driver. bullmq requires Redis. pg-boss uses your existing PostgreSQL database.

inputs.name: connection
inputs.description: Redis URL (bullmq) or PostgreSQL connection string (pg-boss).

inputs.name: concurrency
inputs.description: Maximum number of jobs processed in parallel.

inputs.name: maxAttempts
inputs.description: Maximum retry attempts before a job moves to the dead letter queue.

inputs.name: backoffMs
inputs.description: Initial delay in milliseconds for exponential backoff between retries.

outputs.name: jobResult
outputs.description: Job execution result with id, status, output data, and attempt count.

outputs.name: queueStats
outputs.description: Queue statistics: waiting, active, completed, failed, delayed counts.

events.name: onJobCompleted
events.description: Fired when a job completes successfully.

events.name: onJobFailed
events.description: Fired when a job fails after all retry attempts.

events.name: onJobRetry
events.description: Fired when a job is scheduled for retry.

actions.name: enqueue
actions.description: Add a job to the queue. Returns the job ID.

actions.name: process

params.name: handler
params.description: Async function that processes the job payload.

actions.description: Register a worker function for a queue. Call once at startup.

actions.name: getJob
actions.description: Get the current status and result of a specific job.

actions.name: cancel

params.name: jobId
params.description: ID of the job to cancel.

actions.description: Cancel a waiting or delayed job. Returns false if already active.

llm.constraints: Call process() once per queue at application startup, not inside request handlers. Job payloads must be JSON-serializable. pg-boss creates its own schema in PostgreSQL — no migration needed. BullMQ requires a Redis 6.2+ instance.
